title: LLMs are no longer wholly reliant on the internet
description: Why modern language models still learn from broad corpora, but later training loops now shape much of the behavior readers actually see.
contextSentence: Large language models still begin with broad pretraining on massive text corpora, but the final model is increasingly shaped by instruction data, preference feedback, verifiable tasks, and on-policy loops that arrive after corpus learning.
takeaway: Treat web-scale pretraining as the foundation, not the whole story. Mid-training and post-training loops now supply a large share of the signal that determines how a model follows instructions, avoids unsafe outputs, and solves verifiable tasks.
